text: Continuous integration, often called CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. With CI, every time new code is added, automated builds are triggered. This process compiles the code and runs tests to check for errors. By running tests frequently, developers can catch issues early, often within minutes of making a change. This prevents small bugs from growing into bigger problems later. CI pipelines handle this entire workflow automatically, from building to testing. As a result, teams can work faster and with more confidence, knowing that their code remains stable and reliable.
